workflow.name: Selectively import workflows from GitHub, including nested folders
workflow.description:
This template lets you selectively import n8n workflows from a GitHub repository, even when your repository uses deeply nested folder structures.

Unlike most import workflows, this template does not load everything automatically. Instead, it:
	•	Traverses all nested folders in your repository
	•	Collects every workflow JSON file found
	•	Dynamically generates a selection form
	•	Allows you to choose exactly which workflows to load
	•	Cleans incompatible fields for n8n API compatibility
	•	Creates workflows directly in your instance

This makes it ideal for users who:
	•	Work across multiple n8n instances
	•	Store workflows in structured GitHub directories (e.g. YYYY/MM/)
	•	Want precise control over which workflows are restored
	•	Manage staging, production, or client environments

If you maintain workflows in GitHub and need controlled, selective imports, especially across structured folder hierarchies, this template is built for that use case.

parameters.formDescription: Load select workflows from your chosen GitHub repo.

parameters.content:
## Configure Repo Owner

Set your GitHub repository owner using the form field.

Keep:
• Field Name: `Repo Owner` 
• Label: `Repo Owner`  
• Type: `Dropdown` 

Only update the `default value` and `dropdown options` to match your GitHub account or organisation.  

Do not rename the field or change its type, as other nodes depend on it.

## Create Repository Form Data Structure

Builds the JSON for the dynamic repository selection form (JSON mode).

## How it works 🧠 (Workflow Explanation)
This workflow lets you selectively import n8n workflows from a GitHub repository, including repositories that use nested folders.

Instead of recursion or multiple executions, it uses a queue-based loop inside a single run.

### Here’s what happens:
1. The workflow starts at the root of your repository.
2. Each folder is processed one at a time.
3. Subfolders are added to a queue.
4. Files are collected into a single list.
5. When all folders are processed, a dynamic form is generated.
6. You choose which workflows to import.
7. Selected workflows are cleaned and sent to the n8n API.

### This ensures:
- No recursion
- No duplicate executions
- Full nested-folder support
- Controlled, selective imports

## Setup steps ⚙️
### 1. Configure the Repo Owner in the form node
- Only change the default value and options
- Do not change the field name or type unless updating related variables
### 2. Add your GitHub credentials
- Required to read repository contents
### 3. Add your n8n API key
- Required to create workflows via /api/v1/workflows
### 4. Execute the workflow
- Select the workflows you want to import
- Confirm and run

That’s it. Once credentials are connected, the workflow is ready to use 🚀

## Create Workflow File Form Data Structure

Builds the JSON used to populate the Workflow Selection form.

This section:
• Splits the discovered repository files  
• Creates dynamic SELECT_WORKFLOWS options (Form in JSON mode)  
• Lets the user choose which workflows to import  
• Merges selections back to the original file list  

Only selected workflow files continue downstream.

## Format Workflows

Prepares selected workflow files for safe import into n8n.

This section:
• Verifies the file was successfully retrieved  
• Removes fields incompatible with the n8n API  
• Builds the correct workflow payload  
• Creates or updates the workflow  

Results are merged to ensure a consistent downstream output.

## Structure Success Message

Formats the final result for each processed workflow by creating a clean summary showing whether each workflow was created or failed.

user.bio: Founder and AI automation engineer focused on designing intelligent systems that combine workflow orchestration, data engineering, and AI agents.

data.details:
The IF node can be used to implement binary conditional logic in your workflow. You can set up one-to-many conditions to evaluate each item of data being inputted into the node. That data will either evaluate to TRUE or FALSE and route out of the node accordingly.

This node has multiple types of conditions: Bool, String, Number, and Date & Time.
